summary: French naturalist novelist, author of the 20-novel Rougon-Macquart cycle depicting the Second Empire. Best known for Germinal, Nana, L'Assommoir, and Thérèse Raquin. Childhood friend of Paul Cézanne. Defended Alfred Dreyfus in the open letter 'J'Accuse...!'

works.name: Germinal
works.description: Novel about a miners' strike at the Montsou coal mines.

clues.clue: Étienne Lantier leads a strike at the Montsou mines; set in 'Le Voreux'
examples: Etienne Lantier leads a strike at the Montsou mines in this author's novel Germinal

clues.clue: Étienne kills Catherine's abusive lover Chaval while trapped in a pit
examples: a character kills Catherine's abusive lover Chaval while trapped in a pit

clues.clue: The anarchist Souvarine destroys Le Voreux; his pet rabbit Pologne provides comfort
examples: the rabbit Pologne provides comfort to Souvarine, who destroys Le Voreux

clues.clue: Horse Bataille trapped in a flooded mine
examples: the horse Bataille attempts to survive a flood

works.name: Thérèse Raquin
works.description: Novel about Thérèse and Laurent's murder of her husband Camille.

clues.clue: Laurent bites Camille on the neck and drowns him; paralyzed woman unable to report the murder
examples: a woman who has a stroke is unable to report the murder of a character who bites a man on the neck before drowning

clues.clue: Laurent meets Thérèse while painting Camille's portrait; afterward can only paint things resembling Camille
examples: meets Therese while visiting her home to paint a portrait of her husband

works.name: L'Assommoir
works.description: Novel about the washerwoman Gervaise Macquart's descent into alcoholism.

clues.clue: Gervaise marries the roofer Coupeau after being abandoned by Lantier; bizarre wedding scene at the Louvre
examples: Gervaise marries Coupeau after being abandoned by Lantier; includes a bizarre wedding scene involving a visit to the Louvre

clues.clue: Coupeau falls off a hospital roof and becomes an alcoholic; Gervaise loses her laundry shop
examples: Coupeau ironically falls off the roof of a hospital and becomes an alcoholic

works.name: Nana
works.description: Novel about Gervaise's daughter, a prostitute who dies of smallpox.

clues.clue: Nana is a prostitute/courtesan who dies of smallpox — 'a heap of pus and blood'
examples: finally dies, 'a heap of pus and blood, a shovelful of putrid flesh,' of smallpox

clues.clue: A filthy horse named after Nana is owned by Count Xavier de Vandoeuvres
examples: a filthy one of these creatures is named after the protagonist and is owned by the Count Xavier de Vandoeuvres

works.name: La Bête Humaine
works.description: Novel about railroad employees and murder.

clues.clue: Jacques Lantier drives engine 'La Lison'; Roubaud murders Grandmorin on a train; Flore derails a train
examples: Flore derails a train in an attempt to kill her cousin
Cabuche is wrongly imprisoned for a murder committed by Roubaud on a train
leading him to rekindle his love of murdering by whacking his lover Severine

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Zola's connections to art, the Dreyfus affair, and naturalism.

clues.clue: J'Accuse...! — open letter defending Dreyfus, published in L'Aurore
examples: defended Alfred Dreyfus in the letter 'J'Accuse...!'

clues.clue: Les Rougon-Macquart: 20-novel cycle about the Second Empire
Childhood friend of Cézanne; fictionalized him as Claude Lantier in The Masterpiece, ending their friendship
examples: Paul Cezanne broke off his childhood friendship with this writer after he portrayed Cezanne as the insane protagonist of The Masterpiece

clues.clue: Manet painted his portrait — seated in profile with Japanese screen and Olympia reproduction
examples: This person holds one of his books while seated in profile next to a Japanese folding screen and a reproduction of Olympia

clues.clue: The Experimental Novel — essay applying Claude Bernard's physiology theories to literature; declared 'the metaphysical man is dead'
examples: called for novelists to test, and not assert, their personal sentiments in an essay that applies Claude Bernard's theories

clues.clue: Died of carbon monoxide poisoning from blocked chimney — suspected foul play related to Dreyfus
examples: death of an author and his wife Alexandrine from carbon monoxide poisoning nominally caused by a blocked chimney flue

comprehensive_summary:
Émile Zola (1840-1902) was the foremost French naturalist novelist, author of the 20-novel Rougon-Macquart cycle set during the Second Empire. The cycle traces hereditary traits through the Rougon and Macquart families, beginning with the matriarch Adelaide Fouque (Tante Dide).

Germinal follows Étienne Lantier's experiences leading a miners' strike at the Montsou coal mines (Le Voreux). The anarchist Souvarine, comforted by his pet rabbit Pologne, destroys Le Voreux. Étienne kills Catherine's abusive lover Chaval while trapped in a flooded pit. The horse Bataille struggles to survive underground.

Thérèse Raquin features Laurent biting Camille on the neck and drowning him; a paralyzed woman witnesses but cannot report the murder. L'Assommoir follows the washerwoman Gervaise Macquart, who marries the roofer Coupeau in a scene involving a visit to the Louvre; both descend into alcoholism. Nana, Gervaise's daughter, becomes a courtesan who dies of smallpox. La Bête Humaine centers on railroad employee Jacques Lantier, who drives the engine La Lison; Roubaud murders Grandmorin on a train, and Flore derails a train.

Zola was a childhood friend of Paul Cézanne and fictionalized him as Claude Lantier in The Masterpiece, ending their friendship. Manet painted his portrait in profile with a Japanese screen and Olympia reproduction. Zola wrote The Experimental Novel applying Claude Bernard's physiology to literature. He defended Alfred Dreyfus in the famous open letter 'J'Accuse...!' published in L'Aurore. He died of carbon monoxide poisoning from a blocked chimney — some suspected foul play.
